contractSurface.securityClassification: platform-level design governance, token and component registry updates, design debt analysis, publish/deployment gates, and stack portability readiness evidence
contractSurface.identityNote: FLOW-37 uses design-system-governance as the canonical portability, UI, package, and runtime implementation slug. The current screen is the PlatformOps stack-porting surface backed by the design governance service bundle.
coreGuarantees: Design specs, component maps, tokens, context, and reusable patterns are ingested before arbitration or publication steps.
Design quality, component schema, token consistency, and deployment gates remain hard gates.
Governance records for decisions, versions, and evolution are append-oriented and tenant scoped.
Token library and component catalog updates remain behind database and queue fabric interfaces.
Design debt, architecture score, health score, and cross-design impact calculations are policy guided but bounded by declared configuration.
Stack coupling evidence remains visible to platform operators before portability decisions are recorded.
Tenant scope remains automatic through AsyncLocalStorage.
Provider SDK access remains behind fabric interfaces.
Store-before-event ordering and CloudEvents envelopes remain mandatory.

machineLockedRules: raw design evidence is normalized before downstream analysis
component hierarchy and token extraction retain tenant scope
pattern parsing does not bypass design rule validation
quality and deployment gates cannot be skipped by tenant configuration
schema and token consistency checks run before publication
wrong-role actors cannot publish or fork packages
score calculations produce evidence rather than directly mutating governance state
feedback learning remains asynchronous and tenant scoped
impact analysis cannot relax deployment gate requirements
registry writes happen before change events
version and evolution trackers are append-oriented
publication orchestration remains gated by quality, schema, token, and deployment checks

configurationKeys.description: Tenant-editable FLOW-37 values may tune module naming, stack targets, compatibility thresholds, degraded-stack tolerance, review windows, report mode, and run timeouts without changing tenant isolation, fabric boundaries, gate order, registry append rules, or CloudEvents/outbox behavior.

businessDomainAdaptation.description: Safe tenant adaptations are limited to stack governance naming, target-stack policy, compatibility thresholds, degraded-stack tolerance, review timing, report mode, and run timeout budgets.

edgeCases.name: Acme stack governance hub
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A tunes FLOW-37 for strict platform governance with zero incompatible dimensions and executive reporting.
edgeCases.extensionPoint: module naming, primary stack, target stack, compatibility threshold, report mode
edgeCases.currentImplementation: Design gates, tenant scope, append-only governance logs, and store-before-event ordering remain fixed.

edgeCases.name: Northwind stack readiness console
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant B tunes FLOW-37 for broader operations readiness with a looser compatibility threshold and limited degraded-stack tolerance.
edgeCases.extensionPoint: target stack, degraded stack tolerance, run timeout, review window
edgeCases.currentImplementation: Schema, token, quality, and deployment gates still execute before publication.

edgeCases.name: Tessera design portability lab
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant C tunes FLOW-37 for audit-oriented community review with longer review and run windows.
edgeCases.extensionPoint: review window, report mode, target stack, timeout budget
edgeCases.currentImplementation: No tenant can relax DNA, BFA, role, auth, tenant-scope, or fork provenance rules.

ironRulesMACHINE.description: Fixed invariants that tenant adaptation cannot change.
rules: Task IDs T489 through T515 remain fixed for the design-system-governance implementation bundle.
Factory IDs F1271 through F1329 remain fixed.
Provider SDK access remains behind fabric interfaces.
Vault credential paths remain outside the package and outside tenant docs.
Design evidence normalization precedes arbitration and publication.
Quality, component schema, token consistency, and deployment gates cannot be bypassed.
Design decision, version, and evolution records remain append-oriented.
Registry writes happen before events are emitted.
Tenant scope is resolved through AsyncLocalStorage.
Store-before-event ordering and CloudEvents envelopes remain mandatory.
Marketplace forks must use tenant credentials from Vault.
GitHub Actions and local registry readback are required cascade evidence.

tenantAStackGovernanceHub.description: Tenant A tunes FLOW-37 for strict stack governance.
tenantAStackGovernanceHub.impact: Acme gets a stricter governance hub while gate order, tenant isolation, and fork provenance remain unchanged.
tenantAStackGovernanceHub.testable: Fork CI and live auth checks still pass after tenant.config.json, package metadata, and freedom defaults record these values.

tenantBReadinessConsole.description: Tenant B tunes FLOW-37 for broader operations readiness.
tenantBReadinessConsole.impact: Northwind gets a wider readiness console while deployment gates and registry append behavior stay fixed.
tenantBReadinessConsole.testable: Tenant B fork from Tenant A publishes a distinct package version and keeps CI green.

tenantCPortabilityLab.description: Tenant C tunes FLOW-37 for audit-oriented portability review.
tenantCPortabilityLab.impact: Tessera gets longer review windows and audit reporting while core governance invariants stay locked.
tenantCPortabilityLab.testable: Tenant C fork from Tenant B adapts locally, passes package CI, and preserves auth boundaries.

notPartOfAdaptationSurface.description: Elements that must not change during tenant adaptation.
elements: quality, schema, token, and deployment gate order
marketplace API as the only valid fork origin

passCriteria: Tenant adaptations may change only declared FLOW-37 configuration values or display/module metadata.
FLOW-37 package assembly copies the design-system-governance implementation under the canonical package slug path.
Auth checks still return 401 for missing or cross-tenant token and 403 for wrong-role token after adaptation.
Forked package CI must pass npm install, TypeScript, Jest, and provenance steps.
GitHub repo evidence screenshots and local registry publish/readback must exist at each cascade point.
